headers_or_fields: RES; Deep formation resistivity; A090; AF90; rt_ohm_m
measures: Formation electrical resistivity beyond the shallow invaded zone
hydrate_positive_side: Higher Rt can support hydrate only where clean reservoir, porosity, NMR/core, and elastic evidence agree
hydrate_negative_or_false_side: High Rt is non-unique and can occur without hydrate
primary_hydrate_effect: Hydrate replaces conductive pore water and raises formation resistivity
major_masks_or_false_positives: Free gas; ice or permafrost; carbonate/cement; tight low-porosity rock; shale correction problems; water-resistivity/salinity assumptions; invasion or tool effects; washout
slide_caveat_summary: Can be mimicked by gas, ice, carbonate/cement, tight rock, shale correction, Rw/salinity, invasion, or bad hole
overburden_or_geologic_context: Needs lithology and local formation-water context before interpretation
visual_icon_or_scene: Electric current paths blocked by hydrate-filled pores; horizontal scale low-to-high Rt
model_role: Strong input feature, not a standalone label; interacts with porosity, GR, NMR, Vp, and Vs
source_basis: Chong et al. 2022; Lee and Collett 2011; Haines et al. 2022; header map

measures: NMR-visible pore fluid and porosity response; derived saturation when explicitly supplied
hydrate_positive_side: Reduced mobile-fluid response or NMR-density separation can support pore-filling hydrate
hydrate_negative_or_false_side: Clay-bound water, tool settings, missing NMR, or depth mismatch can confuse response
primary_hydrate_effect: Hydrate occupies pore space and reduces mobile water signal relative to density/core porosity
major_masks_or_false_positives: Shale/clay bound water; poor depth match; missing NMR coverage; target-derived NMR_SAT leakage; tool processing settings
slide_caveat_summary: Can be distorted by clay-bound water, depth mismatch, missing coverage, processing settings, or target leakage
overburden_or_geologic_context: Needs depth alignment to density/core and target provenance
visual_icon_or_scene: Pore-fluid signal shrinking inside a rock pore; lock icon on target-derived NMR_SAT
model_role: Input when NMRPHI is measured; target/calibration when NMR_SAT is derived
source_basis: Chong et al. 2022; Lee and Collett 2011; Haines et al. 2022; header map

hydrate_positive_side: Higher Vp can support hydrate stiffness when paired with Vs and clean reservoir context
hydrate_negative_or_false_side: Very low Vp can indicate gas; high Vp can also come from non-hydrate rock stiffness
primary_hydrate_effect: Hydrate stiffens the sediment frame and raises compressional velocity
major_masks_or_false_positives: Overburden compaction; carbonate/cement; ice; competent lithology; stress; anisotropy; slowness-to-velocity conversion errors
slide_caveat_summary: Can be shifted by compaction, cement/carbonate, ice, lithology, stress, anisotropy, or unit conversion
overburden_or_geologic_context: Must correct for burial and rock type before comparing wells
visual_icon_or_scene: Compression wave entering a rock block; horizontal scale slow-to-fast wave
model_role: Input feature and source for acoustic impedance and elastic moduli
source_basis: Chong et al. 2022; Lee and Collett 2011; Haines et al. 2022; equation docs

hydrate_positive_side: Higher Vs supports frame stiffening and helps separate hydrate from free gas
hydrate_negative_or_false_side: High Vs can also reflect competent non-hydrate lithology or stress effects
primary_hydrate_effect: Hydrate increases rigidity; free gas affects Vp more strongly than Vs
major_masks_or_false_positives: Overburden compaction; cementation; carbonate; ice; competent lithology; stress; anisotropy; missing shear sonic
slide_caveat_summary: Can be shifted by compaction, cement/carbonate, ice, lithology, stress, anisotropy, or missing shear data
overburden_or_geologic_context: Needs overburden and lithology context for cross-well comparisons
visual_icon_or_scene: Shear wave deforming a rock sideways; low-to-high rigidity scale
model_role: Input feature and source for mu-rho, shear modulus, brittleness, and Vp/Vs
source_basis: Chong et al. 2022; Lee and Collett 2011; Haines et al. 2022; equation docs

headers_or_fields: Rho_b; RHOB; Density_gcpcc; Phi_porosity; DPHI; phi_den; rhob_g_cc; density_porosity_vv
hydrate_positive_side: Porous sand with plausible density porosity supports reservoir capacity; density/NMR separation can support hydrate
hydrate_negative_or_false_side: Density alone is weak because hydrate-water density contrast is small
primary_hydrate_effect: Density constrains porosity and elastic calculations more than direct hydrate presence
major_masks_or_false_positives: Overburden compaction; mineralogy; shale; carbonate; coal; unit confusion kg/m3 vs g/cc; borehole washout
slide_caveat_summary: Can be distorted by compaction, mineralogy, shale, carbonate, coal, unit confusion, or washout
overburden_or_geologic_context: Overburden map matters because burial pressure changes density/porosity baseline
visual_icon_or_scene: Rock cube on scale plus pore-volume gauge; porosity scale from tight to open
model_role: Input feature and equation input; derived porosity needs provenance
source_basis: Header map; equation docs; Helmold and LePain 2023; Lee and Collett 2011

measures: Natural gamma radiation used as a lithology and shale-volume indicator
hydrate_positive_side: Lower GR can indicate cleaner sand reservoir where hydrate could occupy pores
hydrate_negative_or_false_side: Low GR is not hydrate; high GR shale can degrade other logs and reduce reservoir quality
primary_hydrate_effect: GR separates reservoir-quality sand from shale-prone non-reservoir intervals
major_masks_or_false_positives: Radioactive minerals; shale laminations; volcanic ash; clean non-hydrate sand; depth mismatch with core lithology
slide_caveat_summary: Can be confused by radioactive minerals, shale laminations, volcanic ash, clean no-hydrate sand, or core-log mismatch
overburden_or_geologic_context: Depositional fairway and stratigraphy control whether low-GR sand is meaningful
visual_icon_or_scene: Clay/shale radiation icon versus clean sand bed; horizontal shale-to-sand scale
model_role: Input feature and reservoir gate, not a hydrate label
source_basis: Header map; Helmold and LePain 2023; Lewis and Collett 2013

measures: Borehole diameter, gauge condition, and washout risk
hydrate_positive_side: In-gauge borehole supports trust in density, neutron, sonic, and resistivity response
hydrate_negative_or_false_side: Caliper is not hydrate evidence; it controls whether other curves can be trusted
primary_hydrate_effect: Caliper is a quality gate, not hydrate evidence
major_masks_or_false_positives: Washout; rugosity; tool standoff; drilling damage; bit-size reference errors; differential-caliper sign convention
slide_caveat_summary: Bad hole can corrupt density, neutron, sonic, and resistivity before ML sees them
overburden_or_geologic_context: Bad-hole zones should be flagged before model fitting
visual_icon_or_scene: Borehole gauge icon; green in-gauge side and red washout side
model_role: QC exclusion feature and missingness/routing flag
source_basis: Chong et al. 2022; header map; runtime validation

measures: Derived elastic ratios from Vp, Vs, and density
hydrate_positive_side: Hydrate-consistent stiffness pattern can support phase interpretation when Vp, Vs, density, and lithology agree
hydrate_negative_or_false_side: Derived features inherit every error from input velocities and density
primary_hydrate_effect: Combines wave speed and density to describe rock stiffness and phase response
major_masks_or_false_positives: Input dependency errors; unit conversion errors; overburden; lithology; gas; shale; carbonate; ice; stress
slide_caveat_summary: Can be wrong if Vp, Vs, density, units, lithology, stress, or bad-hole inputs are wrong
overburden_or_geologic_context: Must inherit provenance from Vp, Vs, and density; cannot be cleaner than inputs
visual_icon_or_scene: Crossplot icon with hydrate/gas/water regions; positive side is multi-log agreement
model_role: Derived feature, not raw measured input
source_basis: Equation docs; Lee and Collett 2011; Haines et al. 2022

measures: Laboratory reservoir quality, lithology, and calibration evidence where available
hydrate_positive_side: Core confirms reservoir quality, calibration, and failure cases
hydrate_negative_or_false_side: Sparse, depth-offset, or poor-quality core can mislead if treated as continuous truth
primary_hydrate_effect: Core anchors log interpretation and tests producibility assumptions
major_masks_or_false_positives: Depth mismatch; sampling bias; core disturbance; scale mismatch; sparse coverage; pressure-core quality
slide_caveat_summary: Can mislead if depth-shifted, disturbed, sparse, biased, or treated as continuous truth
overburden_or_geologic_context: Core intervals must be tied to log depth with offset flags
visual_icon_or_scene: Core plug beside log track; calibration link line
model_role: Calibration and validation, not a direct public-data input
source_basis: Project core-calibration scaffold; Helmold and LePain 2023

headers_or_fields: DEPTH; DEPT; Depth_ft; depth_m; pressure_mpa; temperature_c; future overburden map
measures: Depth, pressure-temperature admissibility, and burial/overburden context
hydrate_positive_side: Inside stability context and reasonable burial trend supports admissibility
hydrate_negative_or_false_side: Stability context is necessary but never proves hydrate by itself
primary_hydrate_effect: Defines where hydrate can exist and how compaction/stress shifts log baselines
major_masks_or_false_positives: Stability alone; local thermal gradient; abnormal pressure; structural position; overburden variation; depth-unit or alignment errors
slide_caveat_summary: Can be overread if stability, overburden, depth units, pressure, temperature, or structure are wrong
overburden_or_geologic_context: Future Alaska North Slope overburden map should adjust density/velocity expectations
visual_icon_or_scene: Vertical depth column plus overburden stack; depth remains un-normalized
model_role: Context feature and split/alignment axis; depth is not normalized like privacy-controlled curves
source_basis: Header map; USGS/Collett sources; overburden docs
